violations.text: The doors to the outside, located in the front entrance area, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

A bag of onions were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Staff moved the onions off the floor during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025

The cutting board in the kitchen is very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2025

The ceiling in the dishwashing area is in poor repair. Coating observed peeling from ceiling. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. -Per interview of staff, a service order has been placed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025

The presence of roaches is not controlled as evidenced by a large accumulation of live roaches observed in dish machine area as well as live and dead activity in bar and dining room areas. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2024
A dipping well was in poor repair (observed with active leak into large bucket underneathe). Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces were observed with accumulation of debris: 1) Underside of shelving in cook line had soil and food splash and residue 2) Underside ares of dish machine has accumulation of soil and biofilm 3) Back side of equipment in cook and prep line has dust and soil debris 4) Underside of storage shelves in back prep area has food residue and soil build up 5) Table, counter tops, and can opener holster observed with food and soil residue Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024
The plumbing for the condensate/excess water runoff of the ice machine is not sufficiently draining into the drain and pooling on the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed in poor repair: 1) Cracks and gaps observed on tiles behind dish machine (roaches observed coming from this area) 2) Grout missing on floor tiles (water and debris build up in missing grout causing pest harborage) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of pooling water, soil residue, and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024
A blender and ice were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024

The presence of roaches is not controlled as evidenced by dead and live activity observed in ware-washing, cook, prep, bar, and dining areas. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024
A dipping well was in poor repair (observed with active leak at base). New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces were observed with an accumulation of debris: 1) All food storage racks in back area had food residue 2) Shelving and counter top surfaces in main cook-prep line had food and other residue 3) Refrigeration, cooking, and storage equipment throughout facility had food and other residue New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored prep areas. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 100 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC called for dishwasher technician to service unit at the time of inspection. Unit to not be used until serviced. All dishes must be sanitized in 3-bay sink until dishwasher is adjusted. New Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023

A speed rack full of potatoes on sheet pans were observed stored right next to a hand sink in the kitchen. Per PIC, storage area is a common place for the speed rack. Establishment to add a splash guard on hand sink to prevent potential contamination. Speed rack was moved at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023

Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. PIC stated normal scoop storage was broken and would be replaced. Scoops were moved to have the handles above ingredients. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023

Multiple refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. Per PIC, gaskets have been ordered. Refrigerator next to stove top to be monitored frequently until gaskets have been replaced. (Bottom refrigerator door keeps popping open during top door use.) New Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023
